In 2010, the projects of the municipality include:[[40]](#cite_note-82)

* The "docks and warehouses," Brownfield site of 5 hectares (12 acres) at the edge of the Saint-Quentin canal, the assignment of which has not yet been determined but which should essentially be devoted to recreation.[[41]](#cite_note-83)
* The rehabilitation of the centre of the Martin–Martine quarter, and in particular the transformation of the green corridor which was originally reserved for an "urban expressway", never realised.[[42]](#cite_note-84)
* The layout of the *Place du 9-Octobre* around the Church of Saint-Géry [Saint Gaugericus]
* The redevelopment of the station quarter in the "exchange hub": A study on the development of the multimodal trade hub of Cambrai was launched in 2007 by the agglomeration community of Cambrai in partnership with the city, the Northern Department and the region, with purpose the station District redevelopment and improvement of [intermodality](/wiki/Intermodal_passenger_transport "Intermodal passenger transport").[[43]](#cite_note-85)
* The relocation of the library to new premises.
* The "modernisation" of the public garden.

On the other hand, the [local urbanism plan](/w/index.php?title=Plan_local_d%27urbanisme&action=edit&redlink=1 "Plan local d'urbanisme (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plan_local_d%27urbanisme "fr:Plan local d'urbanisme")] revolves around eight themes: "Cambrai, an urban centre in the countryside", "Preserve and enhance the natural, rural and agricultural space", "Reclaim and restructure", "Render Cambrai fully in its *urbanity*", "Build upon the city of history", "Save and protect the environment, improve the quality of the living environment", "Ensuring economic and sustainable development", and also "Mastering travel or promoting changes in modes of transport".[[44]](#cite_note-86)

## Toponymy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Toponymy")]

The location is attested as *Camaracum* in the 4th century in the [Peutinger Table](/wiki/Tabula_Peutingeriana "Tabula Peutingeriana") and *Cameracum* (no date). It recognises the Gallo-Roman suffix of Celtic origin [*-acum*](/w/index.php?title=Suffix_of_-acum&action=edit&redlink=1 "Suffix of -acum (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suffixe_-acum "fr:Suffixe -acum")] "place of", "property of", preceded by an element not identified with certainty. [Albert Dauzat](/wiki/Albert_Dauzat "Albert Dauzat") and [Charles Rostaing](/wiki/Charles_Rostaing "Charles Rostaing")[[45]](#cite_note-87) offered the Gallo-Roman type name of a person called Camarus.

This is also found in the anthroponym of [Chambray](/wiki/Chambray "Chambray") (Eure) (*Cambracus* 1011, *Cameragus* to 1025). Variants *Cambarius* and *Camarius* would also explain [Cambayrac](/wiki/Cambayrac "Cambayrac"), [Chambry](/wiki/Chambry,_Aisne "Chambry, Aisne") in Aine and [the same](/wiki/Chambry,_Seine-et-Marne "Chambry, Seine-et-Marne") in Seine-et-Marne, [Chamery](/wiki/Chamery "Chamery"), [Chémery](/wiki/Ch%C3%A9mery "Chémery"), etc. François de Beaurepaire[[46]](#cite_note-88) notes that it may also be a pre-Latin *camar* or *cambar* theme. However, [Xavier Delamarre](/wiki/Xavier_Delamarre "Xavier Delamarre") cited the personal name *Cambarius*, which he considers as based on the Gallic word *cambo-* "curve" (cf. old Irish *camb*, *camm* "curved", "bent" or "twisted"). *Camarus* would be a variant of this Gallic nickname meaning "that which is curved".[[47]](#cite_note-89)

The Cambrai shape is Normanno-Picard with a hard "C", characteristic of the north of the [Joret line](/wiki/Joret_line "Joret line") and therefore corresponds to the form of [Francien](/wiki/Francien_language "Francien language") type Chambray. In addition, the name of the town was written *Cambray* until the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution").

It is known under the name of *Kamerijk* in [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")[[48]](#cite_note-90) and formerly *Kamerich* in [German](/wiki/German_language "German language") and *Camberick/Cambrick* in [English](/wiki/English_language "English language").

## Politics and administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Politics and administration")]

### Political trends and results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Political trends and results")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section needs to be **updated**. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information. *(December 2025)* |

Overall, voting in Cambrai is little different from the national vote, but the abstention rate is often higher: For example, it was 34.86% in the 2005 referendum (against 30.63%); 22.07% in the first round of the 2007 presidential election (compared with 16.23%) and 38.13% in the first round of the legislative elections of 2002 (compared to 35.58%).

Cambresiens voters seem also more cautious with regard to the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") and the French in general: The [referendum](/wiki/Referendum "Referendum") on the ratification of the Treaty on European Union of 1992 was rejected by 53.35%, while at the national level it was approved by a narrow majority of 51.04%. In 2005, the draft law on the ratification of the [Treaty establishing a Constitution for Europe](/wiki/Treaty_establishing_a_Constitution_for_Europe "Treaty establishing a Constitution for Europe") was rejected more widely in Cambrai (59.8% of voted "No") than at the national level (54.67%).

In the [presidential election of 2007](/wiki/2007_French_presidential_election "2007 French presidential election") the results of the second round were very close to national figures: 54.07% for [Nicolas Sarkozy](/wiki/Nicolas_Sarkozy "Nicolas Sarkozy") and 45.93% for [Ségolène Royal](/wiki/S%C3%A9gol%C3%A8ne_Royal "Ségolène Royal"), against respectively 53.06% and 46.94% at the national level. In the first round [Jean-Marie Le Pen](/wiki/Jean-Marie_Le_Pen "Jean-Marie Le Pen") achieved a slightly better result at Cambrai (13.28%) than France (10.44%), while [François Bayrou](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Bayrou "François Bayrou") was in a reversed situation (16.77% against 18.57%). [Arlette Laguiller](/wiki/Arlette_Laguiller "Arlette Laguiller") (2.02%) and [Olivier Besancenot](/wiki/Olivier_Besancenot "Olivier Besancenot") (4.77%) were the only other candidates to exceed 1.5%. In the second round of the [presidential election of 2002](/wiki/2002_French_presidential_election "2002 French presidential election") [Jacques Chirac](/wiki/Jacques_Chirac "Jacques Chirac") arrived largely in the lead in Cambrai as in the rest of the country but Jean-Marie Le Pen's result was higher (21.11% against 17.79%).

In the [legislative elections of 2007](/wiki/2007_French_legislative_election "2007 French legislative election"), [François-Xavier Villain](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-Xavier_Villain "François-Xavier Villain"), the candidate related to the [UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement") and who was also the incumbent Mayor of Cambrai, achieved 57.42% in the first round (48.03% in the constituency). All the other candidates were below the national percentage of their party. For example, the [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") achieved 22.91% against 24.73%, the [Communist Party](/wiki/French_Communist_Party "French Communist Party") at 3.10% compared to 4.29% and the UDF at 6.21% against 7.61%. The decline of the [National Front](/wiki/National_Front_(France) "National Front (France)") was also more marked in Cambrai (4.14%) than elsewhere (4.24%). One finds a situation close to the [2002 elections](/wiki/2002_French_legislative_election "2002 French legislative election").[[49]](#cite_note-91)

In the first round of the [presidential election of 2012](/wiki/2012_French_presidential_election "2012 French presidential election"), the four candidates in the lead in Cambrai were [Nicolas Sarkozy](/wiki/Nicolas_Sarkozy "Nicolas Sarkozy") ([UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement"), 28.29%), [François Hollande](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Hollande "François Hollande") ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)"), 27.56%), [Marine Le Pen](/wiki/Marine_Le_Pen "Marine Le Pen") ([FN](/wiki/National_Front_(France) "National Front (France)"), 20.81%) and [Jean-Luc Mélenchon](/wiki/Jean-Luc_M%C3%A9lenchon "Jean-Luc Mélenchon") ([Left Front](/wiki/Left_Front_(France) "Left Front (France)"), 10.14%) with a turnout of 72.61%. In the second round, François Hollande was in the lead with 50.75% of the vote, with a participation rate of 73.09%.[[50]](#cite_note-92)

### Municipal government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Municipal government")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7f/H%C3%B4tel_de_ville_de_Cambrai_%2827018%29.jpg/250px-H%C3%B4tel_de_ville_de_Cambrai_%2827018%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:H%C3%B4tel_de_ville_de_Cambrai_(27018).jpg)

The city hall, inaugurated in 1932

Cambrai is the [chef-lieu](/wiki/Chef-lieu "Chef-lieu") of one of the six [arrondissements](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Cambrai "Arrondissement of Cambrai") in the [Nord department](/wiki/Nord_(French_department) "Nord (French department)"). Cambrai is also the seat of the [canton of Cambrai](/wiki/Canton_of_Cambrai "Canton of Cambrai"), which consists of 27 communes (including Cambrai).

Since 22 December 1992, Cambrai is the seat of the [Agglomeration Community of Cambrai](/w/index.php?title=Agglomeration_Community_of_Cambrai&action=edit&redlink=1 "Agglomeration Community of Cambrai (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_d%27agglom%C3%A9ration_de_Cambrai "fr:Communauté d'agglomération de Cambrai")] which includes 33 communes and approximately 68,000 inhabitants. The city also adheres to the following intercommunal structures:

* The Intercommunal Association of Sanitation of the Cambrai Agglomeration (SIAC).
* [SIVU](/w/index.php?title=Single-purpose_intercommunal_syndicate&action=edit&redlink=1 "Single-purpose intercommunal syndicate (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syndicat_intercommunal_%C3%A0_vocation_unique "fr:Syndicat intercommunal à vocation unique")] *"Scènes mitoyennes"* ["Adjoining Stages"], created in August 2000, brings together the communes of Cambrai, [Caudry](/wiki/Caudry "Caudry"), [Escaudœuvres](/wiki/Escaud%C5%93uvres "Escaudœuvres") and [Neuville-Saint-Rémy](/wiki/Neuville-Saint-R%C3%A9my "Neuville-Saint-Rémy") with which it harmonises cultural policies.[[51]](#cite_note-93)
* SIVU *"Murs mitoyens"* ["Adjoining Walls"], operational since 1 January 2006 and headquartered in Caudry, its mission is to educate the various planning authorities for the two cities.[[52]](#cite_note-94)
* The Intercommunity Association of the energy of the Cambrésis (SIDEC), which is headquartered at Neuville-Saint-Rémy, brings together the 111 communes in the arrondissement. It concedes the management of the public service energy distribution to [ERDF](/wiki/%C3%89lectricit%C3%A9_R%C3%A9seau_Distribution_France "Électricité Réseau Distribution France") and manages the work of strengthening and concealment of networks.[[53]](#cite_note-95)
* The [Scheme of territorial coherence](/w/index.php?title=Scheme_of_territorial_coherence&action=edit&redlink=1 "Scheme of territorial coherence (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sch%C3%A9ma_de_coh%C3%A9rence_territoriale "fr:Schéma de cohérence territoriale")] (SCoT) of le Cambrésis (via the agglomeration community of Cambrai).
* The Joint Association for the Enhancement of the Upper Scheldt (via the agglomeration community of Cambrai).

Cambrai was merged with the commune of [Morenchies](/wiki/Morenchies "Morenchies") in 1971.

### List of mayors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: List of mayors")]

Since 1945, Cambrai has had three mayors. The city, after having constantly re-elected a [Socialist](/wiki/French_Section_of_the_Workers%27_International "French Section of the Workers' International") mayor from the Liberation until 1977 in the person of Raymond Gernez, has since been administered by mayors from RPR or the related UMP: Jacques Legendre until 1992, then François-Xavier Villain. The latter was elected to the [18th constituency in Nord](/wiki/Nord%27s_18th_constituency "Nord's 18th constituency") on 16 June 2002 and re-elected in 2007 and 2012.

### Judicial and administrative authorities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Judicial and administrative authorities")]

Cambrai was for a short time the seat of the [Parlement](/wiki/Parlement "Parlement") of [French Flanders](/wiki/French_Flanders "French Flanders"), from 1709 until its transfer to [Douai](/wiki/Douai "Douai") in 1713.

The city is now within the jurisdiction of the [Court of Appeal of Douai](/w/index.php?title=Court_of_Appeal_of_Douai&action=edit&redlink=1 "Court of Appeal of Douai (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cour_d%27appel_de_Douai "fr:Cour d'appel de Douai")]. It is the seat of a [high court](/w/index.php?title=Tribunal_de_grande_instance_(France)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tribunal de grande instance (France) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tribunal_de_grande_instance_(France) "fr:Tribunal de grande instance (France)")] whose jurisdiction coincides with the boundaries of the [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissement "Arrondissement"), a [Tribunal d'instance](/wiki/Tribunal_d%27instance "Tribunal d'instance") and an [industrial tribunal](/wiki/Labour_Court_(France) "Labour Court (France)"), installed in the restored [Château de Selles](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Selles&action=edit&redlink=1 "Château de Selles (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Selles "fr:Château de Selles")]. With the reform of the judicial map launched in 2007 the city has lost its [commercial court](/wiki/Tribunal_de_commerce "Tribunal de commerce") and is linked to that of Douai.[[55]](#cite_note-97)

### Environmental policy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Environmental policy")]

The environmental policy is one of the competences of the [Community of the agglomeration of Cambrai](/w/index.php?title=Community_of_the_agglomeration_of_Cambrai&action=edit&redlink=1 "Community of the agglomeration of Cambrai (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_d%27agglom%C3%A9ration_de_Cambrai "fr:Communauté d'agglomération de Cambrai")].

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The [urban unit](/wiki/Urban_unit "Urban unit") (*unité urbaine*) of Cambrai had 45,812 inhabitants in 2022, and the [urban area](/wiki/Functional_area_(France) "Functional area (France)") (*aire urbaine*) 93,174 inhabitants.[[5]](#cite_note-compar-5) In other less populated regions, Cambrai would be an important city, but in [Nord-Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Nord-Pas-de-Calais "Nord-Pas-de-Calais"), densely populated and urbanised, the city and its suburbs come far behind [Lille](/wiki/Lille "Lille") (1,143,125 inhabitants), [Douai](/wiki/Douai "Douai")-[Lens](/wiki/Lens,_Pas-de-Calais "Lens, Pas-de-Calais") (552,682), [Valenciennes](/wiki/Valenciennes "Valenciennes") (399,677), [Dunkirk](/wiki/Dunkirk "Dunkirk") (265,974) and [Maubeuge](/wiki/Maubeuge "Maubeuge") (125,000).

The population of Cambrai increased little over the centuries: Estimated at 10,000 souls in the 15th century, it is, according to a memorandum of intendant who described it as "very diminished", at 12,000 in 1698.[[b 28]](#cite_note-101) At the end of the Revolution, in 1801, it was still only approximately 15,000 inhabitants.

It increased slowly but steadily throughout the 19th century with a net decline in the early 20th century: the birth rate, as everywhere else in France, declined. At the same time, infant mortality remained high (20.3% in 1900, 10.2% on the eve of the war),[[b 29]](#cite_note-102) which explains the low natural growth.

The population growth resumed at a rapid pace between the end of [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") and the beginning of the 1970s (the [Trente Glorieuses](/wiki/Trente_Glorieuses "Trente Glorieuses")), through [natural increase](/wiki/Rate_of_natural_increase "Rate of natural increase") ([baby boomers](/wiki/Post%E2%80%93World_War_II_baby_boom "Post–World War II baby boom") and sharp decline of [infant mortality](/wiki/Infant_mortality "Infant mortality")) and the [rural exodus](/wiki/Rural_flight "Rural flight"), which slowly emptied the villages of Cambrésis of their population (partial) towards the town of Cambrai. This dynamism, however, showed however signs of stalling since 1968: in fact, the progress of jobs (+27.5% from 1952 to 1975) did not follow that of the population (+44.2%).[[b 30]](#cite_note-103)

The curve was brutally reversed by the [1973 oil crisis](/wiki/1973_oil_crisis "1973 oil crisis"). The city's population plummeted from the 1975 census, net migration which was largely positive in the 1960s became negative, while the natural balance, which remains positive, tends to shrink. Many traditional activities have disappeared (chocolate production, brewery, chicory, weaving, metallurgy, etc.), representing several thousand jobs.

#### Population distribution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Population distribution")]

The population distribution of Cambrai (active population aged 15 or more in employment) by [socio-professional category](/w/index.php?title=Professions_and_socioprofessional_categories_in_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Professions and socioprofessional categories in France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Professions_et_cat%C3%A9gories_socioprofessionnelles_en_France "fr:Professions et catégories socioprofessionnelles en France")] showed an under-representation of managers and higher intellectual professionals (10.0%) with the French average (16.3%), in 2017, and vice versa slightly higher proportions of [manual workers](/wiki/Manual_labour "Manual labour") (28.3% compared with 21.5%) and [employees](/wiki/Employee "Employee") (30.2% against 28.5%). The proportion of [intermediate professionals](/w/index.php?title=Profession_interm%C3%A9diaire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Profession intermédiaire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Profession_interm%C3%A9diaire "fr:Profession intermédiaire")] is lower (22.8% against 24.8%). The percentage of [farmers](/wiki/Farmer "Farmer") is naturally low in Cambrai (0.2%).[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)

The distribution of the population over the age of 15 years, and not enrolled in study, showed lower education rates than those of metropolitan France in 2017, most significantly in the higher levels (Baccalauréat or Bac+5 years of study).[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=47 "Edit section: Education")]

Cambrai is the seat of a pool of training divided into three districts (Cambrai-North, Cambrai-South and Cambrai-Le Cateau) and dependent on the [Education Authorities](/w/index.php?title=Direction_des_services_d%C3%A9partementaux_de_l%27%C3%89ducation_nationale&action=edit&redlink=1 "Direction des services départementaux de l'Éducation nationale (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Direction_des_services_d%C3%A9partementaux_de_l%27%C3%89ducation_nationale "fr:Direction des services départementaux de l'Éducation nationale")] of [Nord](/wiki/Nord_(French_department) "Nord (French department)") and the [Academy of Lille](/wiki/Academy_of_Lille "Academy of Lille").[[61]](#cite_note-106)

### Schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=48 "Edit section: Schools")]

The city administers twelve [nursery schools](/w/index.php?title=%C3%89cole_maternelle_in_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "École maternelle in France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89cole_maternelle_en_France "fr:École maternelle en France")], and eleven [elementary schools](/w/index.php?title=%C3%89cole_%C3%A9l%C3%A9mentaire_in_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "École élémentaire in France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89cole_%C3%A9l%C3%A9mentaire_en_France "fr:École élémentaire en France")].

The department manages four [colleges](/w/index.php?title=Coll%C3%A8ge_in_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Collège in France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coll%C3%A8ge_en_France "fr:Collège en France")]: Jules-Ferry, Fénelon,[[62]](#cite_note-fénelon-107) Lamartine and Paul-Duez.

The [Nord-Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Nord-Pas-de-Calais "Nord-Pas-de-Calais") region operates four [high schools](/w/index.php?title=Lyc%C3%A9e_en_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lycée en France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lyc%C3%A9e_en_France "fr:Lycée en France")]: Fénelon,[[62]](#cite_note-fénelon-107) Paul-Duez[[63]](#cite_note-108) and the vocational [Louise de Bettignies](/wiki/Louise_de_Bettignies "Louise de Bettignies")[[64]](#cite_note-109) and Louis-Blériot.[[65]](#cite_note-110) Cambrai also has a private institution, the ensemble of Saint-Luc, bringing together three former private schools merged in September 2009: Collège Jeanne d'Arc, Institution Notre Dame de Grace (college and high school of general and technological education) and the vocational high school La Sagresse.[[66]](#cite_note-saintluc-111)

### University life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: University life")]

Cambrai hosts two branches of the [University of Valenciennes and Hainaut-Cambresis](/wiki/University_of_Valenciennes_and_Hainaut-Cambresis "University of Valenciennes and Hainaut-Cambresis") (UVHC), and [Lille-2](/wiki/Lille_2_University_of_Health_and_Law "Lille 2 University of Health and Law").

UVHC antenna prepares eleven diplomas, which include of DUT, IUP, Master Pro, professional licenses (including "Cultural Actions and Promotion of Heritage" and "Trades of Archaeology") and licenses.[[67]](#cite_note-112)

The branch of Lille-2 prepares to obtain a license "mention droit" or "mention economic and social administration", as well as three professional licenses: For management of small-medium businesses, for transportation of goods and for security professions.[[68]](#cite_note-113)

Finally, Catholic teaching. The whole of Saint Luc de Cambrai[[66]](#cite_note-saintluc-111) was a centre of higher education (Sup'Sagesse) from Bac+1 to Bac+5: BTS optician, BTS insurance, NRC, MUC, AG and AM, two professional licenses ("Operational Marketing Manager" and "Contingency Insurance and Management of Goods") and finally a Professional Master of "Entrepreneurial Strategy and Management", opened to the entrepreneurs.

With nearly 500 students in alternation, learning or school track, Sup'Sagesse[[66]](#cite_note-saintluc-111) is a real city centre campus, incorporating a boarding school.

Other institutions of higher education in Cambrai are the [Ecole Supérieure of Art of Cambrai](/w/index.php?title=%C3%89cole_sup%C3%A9rieure_d%27art_de_Cambrai&action=edit&redlink=1 "École supérieure d'art de Cambrai (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89cole_sup%C3%A9rieure_d%27art_de_Cambrai "fr:École supérieure d'art de Cambrai")][[69]](#cite_note-114) and the Institute of Nursing Education.

Among the [alumni](/wiki/Alumnus "Alumnus") of the [École des Beaux-Arts](/wiki/%C3%89cole_des_Beaux-Arts "École des Beaux-Arts") of Cambrai are Marie-Anne and Ludovic Belleval who are now both the [Lamour Mill](/wiki/Lamour_Mill,_Briastre "Lamour Mill, Briastre")'s [owners](/wiki/Ownership "Ownership") and [curators](/wiki/Curator "Curator") in [Briastre](/wiki/Briastre "Briastre").

## Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Health")]

Medical density is quite good at Cambrai, when compared with the regional and national averages. In the agglomeration community there are 2.04 general practitioners per 1,000 inhabitants (Nord Pas-de-Calais region and metropolitan France 1.65), and 1.98 specialist doctors per 1,000 inhabitants (region: 1.39, metropolitan France: 1.74).[[70]](#cite_note-115)

The Saint-Julien hospital, which housed the poor and the sick, was founded in 1070.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Today it remains as a chapel adjoining the municipal theatre. Over the following centuries, other hospitals were founded: The Saint-Lazare Hospital for lepers, the Charité Hospital, Saint-Jean Hospital, the Saint Jacques au Bois Hospital to welcome the pilgrims, the general hospice of La Charité founded in 1752 to accommodate the elderly, beggars and the marginalised.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

After [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the construction of a modern hospital was envisaged.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The Central Hospital of Cambrai[[71]](#cite_note-116) has a capacity of 770 beds and 108 seats. It employs a staff of 150 officers and has a non-medical staff of 1,200. Three annex buildings are reserved for medium and long stay, maternity (1982) and Psychiatry (1983–1884) patients. A Nursing Training Institute was opened in 1967.

The work for the construction of the present site began in 1959, the installation of the patients being carried out between 1966 and 1968.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In 2007, the work to modernise and expand the central hospital was undertaken.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The 2010 prize list of "the safest hospitals" places the Central Hospital of Cambrai as the 11th best in the national ranking.[[72]](#cite_note-117)

Cambrai has three private clinics: The Sainte-Marie Clinic, Saint-Roch clinic and the Cambrésis Clinic.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: Economy")]

Cambrai is the seat of the [Chamber of Commerce and Industry of the Cambrésis](/w/index.php?title=Chamber_of_Commerce_and_Industry_of_the_Cambr%C3%A9sis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chamber of Commerce and Industry of the Cambrésis (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_du_Cambr%C3%A9sis "fr:Chambre de commerce et d'industrie du Cambrésis")]. In April 2007, it decided to merge with the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Arras, a decision which was called into question on 4 October 2007, by the Ministry of Supervision of the Chambers of Commerce and Industry.[[73]](#cite_note-118)

As a result of the planned closure in 2012 [Airbase 103 of Cambrai-Epinoy](/w/index.php?title=Airbase_103_of_Cambrai-Epinoy&action=edit&redlink=1 "Airbase 103 of Cambrai-Epinoy (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base_a%C3%A9rienne_103_Cambrai-%C3%89pinoy "fr:Base aérienne 103 Cambrai-Épinoy")], Cambrai and [Cambrésis](/wiki/Cambr%C3%A9sis "Cambrésis") are classified from 1 January 2010 and until 31 December 2012 in the free zone "Zone of Defence Restructuring", allowing enterprises to create, settle or develop tax and social security exemptions.[[74]](#cite_note-119)

### Economic history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=52 "Edit section: Economic history")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Sucrerie_escaudoeuvres.jpg/250px-Sucrerie_escaudoeuvres.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sucrerie_escaudoeuvres.jpg)

An example of the development of the food industry in the nineteenth century, the [Escaudœuvres](/wiki/Escaud%C5%93uvres "Escaudœuvres") sugar factory, deemed the largest in Europe

As early as the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), Cambrai was both an agricultural market for its region, which produces mainly cereals and wool, and a centre of weaving (sheets, [woad](/wiki/Isatis_tinctoria "Isatis tinctoria"), cloth, linen, chiffon). This dual role would persist for a long time. The drapery declined at the end of the 13th century but it was replaced by [batiste](/wiki/Batiste "Batiste"), a speciality of the city, which experienced its greatest trend in the 17th century.[[b 31]](#cite_note-120) Production declined, in turn, in the following century, but in 1775 there were still 58,000 pieces of batiste marked to Cambrai.[[b 32]](#cite_note-121)

Economic activity declined under the [First Empire](/wiki/First_French_Empire "First French Empire") due to the wars and the British [blockade](/wiki/Blockade "Blockade"). In the 19th century, the textile industry remained as the dominant activity of the city, with 2,546 workers, men, women and children, in 1848.[[b 33]](#cite_note-HC222-122) Batiste made the bulk of Cambrai's trade with other productions such as soap and refined sea salt. The [food industry](/wiki/Food_industry "Food industry") grew: Brasserie, chicory; the [Bêtise de Cambrai](/wiki/B%C3%AAtise_de_Cambrai "Bêtise de Cambrai") was invented in 1850.[[b 33]](#cite_note-HC222-122)

In the 19th century, the city was industrialised, especially when compared to its neighbours. Municipal officials often refused the installation of new factories, on behalf of sanitation or lack of space. The [Sucrerie centrale de Cambrai](/wiki/Sucrerie_centrale_de_Cambrai "Sucrerie centrale de Cambrai") was created in 1872, by Jules Linard[[75]](#cite_note-123) on the territory of the town of [Escaudœuvres](/wiki/Escaud%C5%93uvres "Escaudœuvres"). However, according to the census of 1886, industry was supporting more than 9,000 people, while agriculture used only 2,000 at most. The city mainly developed its commercial function: At the beginning of the 20th century the branch of the [Bank of France](/wiki/Banque_de_France "Banque de France") from Cambrai ranked 12th in France, before Nice and Toulouse.[[b 34]](#cite_note-124)

### Business and shops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=53 "Edit section: Business and shops")]

There are four zones and parks of activity in the agglomeration:

* The industrial zone of Cantimpré, to the south-west of the city.
* The Actipole Park, on the edge of the A2 autoroute 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) west of Cambrai, offers a total area of 97 hectares (240 acres). As it is completely occupied, it is being extended.
* The zone of Fontaine-Notre-Dame, a kilometre from the A26 autoroute, offers a total area of 75 hectares (190 acres).
* The zone of South Cambrai Proville, one kilometre to the south of Cambrai, on the [RN 44](/w/index.php?title=Route_nationale_44_(France)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Route nationale 44 (France) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Route_nationale_44_(France) "fr:Route nationale 44 (France)")], in the direction of Saint-Quentin, is devoted to the commercial areas over an area of 40 hectares (99 acres).

The economy of Cambrai is based on four pillars:

* [Agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture"), which occupies a third of the territory of the commune.
* The [agri-food industry](/wiki/Agribusiness "Agribusiness"), which occupies an important place in the economy of the commune (confectionery, candy, dairy, etc.) due to strong agricultural activity ([intensive farming](/wiki/Intensive_farming "Intensive farming") of [livestock](/wiki/Animal_husbandry "Animal husbandry") and [cereals](/wiki/Cereal "Cereal")) in the arrondissement (80% of the area).
* Logistics, benefitting from the situation of the commune of Cambrai in the heart of the triangle London–Paris–Benelux and the intersection of two motorways, tends to be concentrated in the western part of the agglomeration. Thus all of the 97 hectares (240 acres) of the Actipôle Raillencourt zone of activity located 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) west of Cambrai on the edge of the A2 autoroute are now used, partly by distribution undertakings, for a thousand jobs around. The extensions Actipole 2 and 3 are underway or study.
* [Textile](/wiki/Textile_industry "Textile industry"), found predominantly in the rest of the arrondissement ([Caudry](/wiki/Caudry "Caudry"), [Villers-Outréaux](/wiki/Villers-Outr%C3%A9aux "Villers-Outréaux"), etc.) is represented in Cambrai by clothing and linens. The Cambrai region is associated with those of [Calais](/wiki/Calais "Calais") and the [Métropole Européenne de Lille](/wiki/M%C3%A9tropole_Europ%C3%A9enne_de_Lille "Métropole Européenne de Lille") within the Up-Tex [competitiveness cluster](/w/index.php?title=P%C3%B4le_de_comp%C3%A9titivit%C3%A9_en_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pôle de compétitivité en France (page does not exist)"), specialised in textile products high performance and customisation.[[76]](#cite_note-125)

The Central Hospital of Cambrai, the commune of Cambrai, [Auchan](/wiki/Auchan "Auchan"), Les Papillons blancs, [Cora](/wiki/Cora_(hypermarket) "Cora (hypermarket)"), TANIS (chemistry, rubber, plastic), the *Compagnie des Engrenages et Réducteurs Messiaen Durand* (mechanical equipment) and the departmental fire and rescue service were, in order, the eight major employers in the town, in 2008.[[77]](#cite_note-126)

### Perspectives

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=54 "Edit section: Perspectives")]

The future [Seine-Scheldt link](/wiki/Seine%E2%80%93Nord_Europe_Canal "Seine–Nord Europe Canal"), the commissioning of which is scheduled for 2016,[[78]](#cite_note-127) is today one of the most promising elements of economic development to the city and the area of Cambrai. The [multimodal](/wiki/Combined_transport "Combined transport") platform of activities of [Marquion](/wiki/Marquion "Marquion"), 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) west of Cambrai and on 156 hectares (390 acres), should be employed to host European logistics and distribution centres and agro-food industries.[[79]](#cite_note-128)

### Income of the population and taxation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=55 "Edit section: Income of the population and taxation")]

In 2009, the median [taxable household income](/w/index.php?title=Revenu_fiscal_par_m%C3%A9nage_en_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Revenu fiscal par ménage en France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Revenu_fiscal_par_m%C3%A9nage_en_France "fr:Revenu fiscal par ménage en France")] was €15,302, which ranks Cambrai at 25,917th place among the 31,604 communes with more than 50 households in metropolitan France.[[80]](#cite_note-129)

In 2008, 263 Cambrai tax households performed the [solidarity tax on wealth](/wiki/Solidarity_tax_on_wealth "Solidarity tax on wealth"), for a mean wealth of €1.665 million and an average tax amount of €5,017.[[81]](#cite_note-130)

### Employment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=56 "Edit section: Employment")]

According to the results of the 2017 Census, the economically [active population](/wiki/Workforce "Workforce") aged 15 and over of the commune included 14,454 people, 24.3% were [unemployed](/wiki/Unemployment "Unemployment") (France: 13.9%).[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)

The distribution of jobs by [sector of activity](/wiki/Business_sector "Business sector") shows the predominant weight (almost 90%) of the [tertiary sector](/wiki/Tertiary_sector_of_the_economy "Tertiary sector of the economy"). This distribution reflects the role which the administrative and commercial centre plays in the commune for the surrounding countryside.

Distribution of jobs by fields of activity (end of 2015)[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)[[82]](#cite_note-France-131)

| Domain | Agriculture | Industry | Construction | Commerce | Services |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cambrai | 0.1% | 6.6% | 3.7% | 36.0% | 53.7% |
| National average | 1.1% | 13.9% | 6.0% | 46.5% | 32.5% |

The distribution of jobs by socio-professional categories shows under-representation of "executives and intellectual professions", and "farmers", as well as an over-representation of the "intermediary professions" and "employees".

Distribution of employment by socio-professional categories (2017 Census)[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)[[82]](#cite_note-France-131)

| Category | Farmers | Artisans, merchants, business leaders | Executives, intellectual professions | Intermediate professions | Employees | Workers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cambrai | 0.2% | 4.6% | 10.0% | 22.8% | 30.2% | 28.3% |
| National average | 1.3% | 6.0% | 16.2% | 24.8% | 28.5% | 21.5% |

The journeys to work are mostly by car (75.9%, France 70.5%) and one can note the low weight of public transport (5.8% against an average of 15.1% in France).[[38]](#cite_note-complet-80)

## Culture and heritage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=57 "Edit section: Culture and heritage")]

### Cultural events and festivities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=58 "Edit section: Cultural events and festivities")]

On 15 August is the communal festival of Cambrai, and one of the highlights of the popular local life. This great festival (or [Ducasse](/w/index.php?title=Ducasse_(festival)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ducasse (festival) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ducasse "fr:Ducasse")]) extends for ten days in the *Place de l'Hôtel de ville* [City Hall Square]. The day of 15 August is punctuated by the traditional parade of the giants [Martin and Martine](/w/index.php?title=Martin_and_Martine&action=edit&redlink=1 "Martin and Martine (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martin_et_Martine "fr:Martin et Martine")], symbols of the city, and a fireworks display. This festival, originally a procession which took place the day after [Trinity Sunday](/wiki/Trinity_Sunday "Trinity Sunday"), dates back to 1220. It was embellished over time with fireworks, banquets and cavalcades, and was regarded as one of the seven wonders of the [Cambrésis](/wiki/Cambr%C3%A9sis "Cambrésis"). Over the centuries the festival has changed, reflecting the concerns of the contemporary: Since attachment to France it has been celebrated on 15 August, festival of the [Assumption](/wiki/Assumption_of_Mary "Assumption of Mary"), in response to the wish of [Louis XIII](/wiki/Louis_XIII "Louis XIII") to devote the Kingdom to the Virgin; in 1790 it celebrated the [Declaration of the Rights of Man](/wiki/Declaration_of_the_Rights_of_Man_and_of_the_Citizen "Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen") and the [Fête de la Fédération](/wiki/F%C3%AAte_de_la_F%C3%A9d%C3%A9ration "Fête de la Fédération"); in 1802, with the signature of the [concordat](/wiki/Concordat "Concordat"), the image of Our Lady of Grace was again carried in procession, reinforced by the bust of [Fénelon](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_F%C3%A9nelon "François Fénelon"). Under the [First Empire](/wiki/First_French_Empire "First French Empire") it celebrated his Imperial Majesty [Napoleon](/wiki/Napoleon "Napoleon"). In the 19th century, finally, interests turned more to local life, and the progress of science and industry.[[a 9]](#cite_note-132)

Musically, the town of Cambrai receives two festivals. Firstly, the *Juventus* classical music festival. The Juventus association mark young talented European soloists. They are appointed, if they accept it, "Juventus Winners" during their first participation in a Juventus festival. Every summer the old and the new winners gather for a fortnight at the festival to prepare chamber music concerts in exceptional conditions. *Juventus*, established in 1991 at the [Royal Saltworks at Arc-et-Senans](/wiki/Royal_Saltworks_at_Arc-et-Senans "Royal Saltworks at Arc-et-Senans"), was set at Cambrai from 1998 with the help of the General Council of the department.[[83]](#cite_note-133) Meanwhile, [*BetiZFest*](/w/index.php?title=BetiZFest&action=edit&redlink=1 "BetiZFest (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/BetiZFest "fr:BetiZFest")] is an alternative music festival, which has been organised since 2003. It is organised during the month of April. *Les Féodales* is a street show which represents the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"). The last edition took place in 2008.[[84]](#cite_note-134)

The Cambrai city hall is the national headquarters of the Union for the horse breed of the "[Trait du Nord](/wiki/Trait_du_Nord "Trait du Nord")". The Trait du Nord national competition is traditionally held during the last weekend of July at the *Palais des Grottes*. Bringing together the elite of the breed, it ends on Sunday afternoon on the *Place de l'hôtel de Ville* with a grand parade, the most important festive presentation in France of a breed of draft horses.[[85]](#cite_note-135)

### Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=59 "Edit section: Media")]

The *[La Voix du Nord](/wiki/La_Voix_du_Nord_(daily) "La Voix du Nord (daily)")* regional daily publishes a local edition. The *L'Observateur du Cambrésis* is a weekly local news and announcements. The municipal newspaper *Le Cambrésien* is distributed in all the mailboxes of the city.[[86]](#cite_note-136)

Cambrai is part of the territory served by radio BLC, a community radio station whose programmes are broadcast from [Caudry](/wiki/Caudry "Caudry"). The inhabitants of Cambrai also have, in addition to some national radio stations, the programmes of [France Bleu Nord](/wiki/France_Bleu_Nord "France Bleu Nord"), [Chérie FM](/wiki/Ch%C3%A9rie_FM "Chérie FM") Cambrai and [RFM Nord](/wiki/RFM_(French_radio_station) "RFM (French radio station)").[[87]](#cite_note-137)

The city is covered by the programmes of [France 3 Nord](/wiki/France_3_Nord-Pas-de-Calais "France 3 Nord-Pas-de-Calais") and the national [DTT](/wiki/Television_in_France "Television in France") channels. It also received the regional channel of [Wéo](/w/index.php?title=W%C3%A9o&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wéo (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/W%C3%A9o "fr:Wéo")]. Oxygen TV is a [web TV](/wiki/Web_television "Web television") channel "100% of Cambrai" devoted to local information.[[88]](#cite_note-138)

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=60 "Edit section: Religion")]

The people of Cambrai have places of [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church"), [Protestant](/wiki/Protestantism "Protestantism") and [Muslim](/wiki/Islam "Islam") worship.

Cambrai is the seat of a Catholic [archbishopric](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop"), [suffragan](/wiki/Suffragan_bishop "Suffragan bishop") of Lille since 29 March 2008. Until then, it was the opposite situation with the [Archdiocese of Cambrai](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Cambrai "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Cambrai") as the metropolitan archdiocese and Lille and [Arras](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Arras "Roman Catholic Diocese of Arras") as its suffragans. The Archdiocese of Cambrai includes the arrondissements of Cambrai, Valenciennes and Avesnes-sur-Helpe. The [deanery](/wiki/Deanery "Deanery") of Cambrai brings together 13 churches[[89]](#cite_note-139) grouped into two [parishes](/wiki/Parish "Parish"):[[90]](#cite_note-140) Our Lady of Grace[[91]](#cite_note-141) and Saint Vaast-Saint Géry.[[92]](#cite_note-142)

The [Baptist](/wiki/Baptists "Baptists") community has an Evangelical Baptist Church,[[93]](#cite_note-143) just as there is a parish of the [Reformed Church of France](/wiki/Reformed_Church_of_France "Reformed Church of France").[[94]](#cite_note-144)

The Moroccan cultural and religious association of Cambrai manages the Al Mohssinine [Mosque](/wiki/Mosque "Mosque") of Escaudain.[[95]](#cite_note-145)

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=61 "Edit section: Sports")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Centre_nautique_Cambrai.JPG/250px-Centre_nautique_Cambrai.JPG)](/wiki/File:Centre_nautique_Cambrai.JPG)

The Liberté Swimming Centre

Cambrai has over a hundred clubs or sporting associations, including the [Cambrai Hockey Club](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai_Hockey_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cambrai Hockey Club (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cambrai_Hockey_Club "fr:Cambrai Hockey Club")][[96]](#cite_note-146) playing in the Women's [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") Championship of France, as well as the team of [Cambrai Volley Élan du Cambrésis](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai_Volley-Ball&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cambrai Volley-Ball (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cambrai_Volley-Ball "fr:Cambrai Volley-Ball")][[97]](#cite_note-147) which plays in the League (2nd division) and is the only professional club of le Cambrésis.

Facilities include six gymnasiums, two swimming pools, of which the Liberty Swimming Centre was rebuilt and reopened in 2008, the Arsenal de Balagny, which was built between 1581 and 1595, abandoned by the army in 1967 and then rehabilitated as a gym, a leisure centre, a hockey stadium, a rugby stadium and many football pitches, including the Liberty Stadium, home of [AC Cambrai](/wiki/AC_Cambrai "AC Cambrai").[[98]](#cite_note-148)

Cambrai has a rowing club that goes under the name of *Union Nautique de Cambrai*. The club is regularly present to the Rowing French Championships. In the early 2000s, one of its feminine team members made it to the World Championships. Since then, the club has been in the phase of beginning a new cycle based on renewed team members, especially youngsters.
Every year, the club is home to the Regattas of Cambrai, during which clubs from Northern France gather for sprint-races on a 1000m distance.[[99]](#cite_note-149)

The game of [billon](/w/index.php?title=Billon_(game)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Billon (game) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billon_(jeu) "fr:Billon (jeu)")] is practiced traditionally in regions of Cambrai and Douai.

Cambrai was the departure point for Stage 4 of the [2004 Tour de France](/wiki/2004_Tour_de_France "2004 Tour de France")[[100]](#cite_note-150) and once again the departure point of Stage 4 in the [2010 Tour de France](/wiki/2010_Tour_de_France "2010 Tour de France").[[101]](#cite_note-151) Cambrai hosted the finish of Stage 4 in the [2015 Tour de France](/wiki/2015_Tour_de_France "2015 Tour de France"), on 7 July, with a route from [Seraing](/wiki/Seraing "Seraing").

In 2010, the newspaper *[L'Équipe](/wiki/L%27%C3%89quipe "L'Équipe")* ranked Cambrai among the top five cities as the most sporty in France with more than 20,000 inhabitants, along with [Lorient](/wiki/Lorient "Lorient"), [Colmar](/wiki/Colmar "Colmar"), [Antibes](/wiki/Antibes "Antibes") and [Tarbes](/wiki/Tarbes "Tarbes"). The special prize of "Sport and Disability" was awarded to the city for its access to sport for people with disabilities.[[102]](#cite_note-152)

### French sartorial heritage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=62 "Edit section: French sartorial heritage")]

The city was a pivotal center of [mulquinerie](/wiki/Mulquinerie "Mulquinerie").

### Sites and monuments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cambrai&action=edit&section=63 "Edit section: Sites and monuments")]
